types.name: Park
types.description: Public green space — from pocket parks to regional parks. Grass, trees, paths, benches.
types.distinct_quality: Living landscape that changes with seasons. The oldest form of public activation.
types.activation_potential: Gathering, performance, gardening, play, rest, protest, ceremony

types.name: Library
types.description: Public library building and grounds. Indoor conditioned space with information access.
types.distinct_quality: The only public space where you can exist without spending money, with access to knowledge and climate control.
types.activation_potential: Learning, digital access, community meeting, maker space, job search, shelter

types.name: Recreation Center
types.description: Public indoor facility with gymnasium, pool, meeting rooms.
types.distinct_quality: Climate-controlled public space designed for physical activity and gathering.
types.activation_potential: Sports, fitness, youth programs, senior programs, community events, voting

types.name: Public Swimming Pool
types.description: Outdoor or indoor public pool.
types.distinct_quality: Water access as public right. One of few public spaces where bodies of all ages share space.
types.activation_potential: Swimming, water safety, youth programs, therapeutic use, social mixing

types.name: Plaza / Town Square
types.description: Hard-surfaced public gathering space, often at civic center.
types.distinct_quality: Designed for assembly. The democratic floor.
types.activation_potential: Markets, performances, protests, ceremonies, daily gathering

types.name: Sidewalk
types.description: Public pedestrian right-of-way.
types.distinct_quality: The most ubiquitous public space. Everyone walks. The sidewalk is where city life happens.
types.activation_potential: Walking, street vending, café seating, art installation, wayfinding

types.name: Public Parking Lot
types.description: Municipal surface parking lot.
types.distinct_quality: Vast public land devoted to storing cars. Enormous activation potential if reimagined.
types.activation_potential: Markets, drive-in events, temporary parks, housing, solar farms, festivals

types.name: School Grounds
types.description: Public school buildings, yards, gymnasiums, fields — available outside school hours.
types.distinct_quality: Temporal space: public for students during day, potentially public for community after hours.
types.activation_potential: After-hours community use, weekend markets, adult education, recreation

types.name: Transit Station / Platform
types.description: Bus stop, train station, subway platform, ferry terminal.
types.distinct_quality: Space of transition — millions pass through daily. Captive attention.
types.activation_potential: Art, music, retail, information, services, community connection

types.name: Cemetery
types.description: Public burial ground and memorial landscape.
types.distinct_quality: Deepest temporal layer — centuries of community memory inscribed in stone.
types.activation_potential: History walks, ecology (old-growth trees), contemplation, memorial events

types.name: Waterfront / Pier / Boardwalk
types.description: Public access to water edge — rivers, lakes, ocean.
types.distinct_quality: Edge space where land meets water. Historically working, now transitioning.
types.activation_potential: Fishing, walking, markets, performances, kayaking, ecological restoration

types.name: Underpass / Overpass
types.description: Space beneath highway overpasses or bridges.
types.distinct_quality: Covered outdoor space created by infrastructure. Often neglected. Shelter from rain.
types.activation_potential: Markets, skate parks, murals, lighting installations, gathering

types.name: Public Housing Common Area
types.description: Courtyards, lobbies, community rooms, stairwells in public housing developments.
types.distinct_quality: Shared space within residential context. Intimate public — neighbors share it daily.
types.activation_potential: Community organizing, childcare, elder socializing, gardens, art

types.name: Municipal Vacant Lot
types.description: City-owned empty parcel — cleared, overgrown, or abandoned.
types.distinct_quality: Blank canvas. No existing use to displace. Maximum activation potential.
types.activation_potential: Gardens, tiny houses, pop-up events, art installations, micro-parks

types.name: Government Building Public Area
types.description: City hall atrium, courthouse lobby, DMV waiting area, post office.
types.distinct_quality: Indoor public space with captive audience — people who MUST be there.
types.activation_potential: Art, information, services, comfort, dignity improvements

types.name: Fire Station
types.description: Fire department building and apparatus bay.
types.distinct_quality: Neighborhood anchor present 24/7. Already trusted by community.
types.activation_potential: Community education, emergency preparedness, neighborhood gathering

types.name: Median / Traffic Island
types.description: Paved or landscaped area between traffic lanes.
types.distinct_quality: Micro-space surrounded by movement. Underestimated as activation site.
types.activation_potential: Micro-gardens, art, wayfinding, calming, tree planting

types.name: Public Building Rooftop
types.description: Rooftop of a publicly owned building.
types.distinct_quality: Elevated perspective. Sky access. Often completely unused.
types.activation_potential: Gardens, solar, observation, events, green infrastructure

types.name: Alley / Easement
types.description: Service alley, utility easement, right-of-way behind buildings.
types.distinct_quality: Interstitial space. Back-of-house turned public.
types.activation_potential: Green alleys, murals, lighting, pedestrian cut-throughs, gardens

types.name: Reservoir / Water Treatment Facility Grounds
types.description: Public water infrastructure with surrounding land.
types.distinct_quality: Large land parcels, often scenic, usually closed to public. Enormous potential.
types.activation_potential: Walking trails, ecology, education about water systems, passive recreation

types.name: decommissioned-rail-corridor
types.description: Discovered in Chicago

types.name: Island Park / Detached Public Land
types.description: Public land accessible only by water. Temporal boundary (ferry schedule) creates distinct activation rhythm. Former military/institutional use gives deep temporal layer.
